**Acceptance Criteria**
Fail. The shared helper exists in [labels.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/labels.rs:136) and the five explicitly identified sites now call it, e.g. [issue.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs:36), [batch_create.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/commands/batch_create.rs:553), [breakdown.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/commands/breakdown.rs:607), and [validate.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:838).

Blocking issue: this is not a single shared helper. [template.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/commands/template.rs:995) still defines its own private `type_label_value`, and [template.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/commands/template.rs:999) still hard-codes `ns == "type"`. [template.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/commands/template.rs:947) also duplicates type-label filtering with `ns != "type"`. That violates: “A single shared helper ... is the only place that knows the `type:` label encoding.”

**Architecture / Separation**
No storage or CLI boundary violation found in the reviewed diff. The helper is in `labels.rs`, and command code mostly consumes it. The remaining concern is duplicated domain label knowledge inside `commands/template.rs`.

**Functional Paradigm**
The new helper uses iterator combinators and is pure. No new imperative debt found in the changed sites.

**Correctness / Invariants**
No new atomic-write, event-log, gate-semantics, assignee, PID, or unsafe-code issue found in this change. The centralization invariant for type-label parsing is not met because `template.rs` still owns a parallel implementation.
